column1: # FF1 ground-truth set — correlation precision.  Columns: product
# Each CVE appears twice: one product string at a version it REALLY applies to
# (applies=true) and one at a version it does NOT (applies=false
# out of the affected range). "applies" is the NVD / vendor-advisory truth —
# version constraints were looked up per CVE (see nvd_constraint sources below).
# The harness feeds `product` to argus_vuln::correlate_product and checks whether
# `cve_id` is reported and at what match_confidence
# confidence tier. This isolates one question: does the confidence label predict
# Affected versions are chosen safely INSIDE the affected range (not on a
# boundary) so the measurement is about confidence-vs-precision
# Two tiers exist in the catalog:
# A version-blind entry reports the CVE regardless of version
# row is a false positive BY CONSTRUCTION — which is exactly why it carries Low
# confidence. Honest exception: MySQL's catalog range (5.1.0..=5.6.5) spans
# multiple branches with different per-branch fixes
# falls in range and is reported at HIGH confidence — a genuine High-confidence
# false positive from a too-coarse range
# Run against your own set: cargo run -p argus-eval -- ff1 <file.csv>
# --- expanded-catalog entries (verify the newly added records correlate) ---
